metadata.title: Kampung Iman Grounded Learning Pack
safetyNotes: Use Qur'an references and widely known sahih hadith only.
Do not depict prophets, angels, sahabah, or any sacred figures.
Avoid faces on characters or use back-view/silhouette/featureless low-poly villagers.
Keep all religious content gentle, non-sectarian, and parent-reviewable.

quizQuestions.prompt: A friend feels sad. What is a beautiful way to speak?
choices: Use kind words and comfort them
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Wonderful. The Qur'an teaches us to speak good words to people.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Try again with gentle speech. Kind words help hearts feel safe.

quizQuestions.prompt: How should we treat our parents or caregivers?
choices: Only when we want a treat
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Yes. The Qur'an teaches excellent kindness to parents.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Remember: respect and gentle service are beautiful deeds.

quizQuestions.prompt: What should you love for others?
choices: The good things you love for yourself
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Great. A believer loves good for others too.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Think about sharing goodness, not only keeping it for yourself.
quizQuestions.source: Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim — love for your brother what you love for yourself

quizQuestions.prompt: You see a banana peel on the walkway. What can be a charity?
choices: Move it safely so no one slips
Step over it and leave it
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Excellent. Removing harm from the road is a charity.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Try the helpful choice that protects others from harm.
quizQuestions.source: Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim — removing harmful things from the road is charity

quizQuestions.prompt: What short du'a asks Allah for more knowledge?
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Beautiful. It means: My Lord, increase me in knowledge.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Look for the Qur'anic du'a about growing in knowledge.
quizQuestions.source: Qur'an 20:114 — My Lord, increase me in knowledge

quizQuestions.prompt: Surah Al-'Asr reminds us that time is precious. What should we fill it with?
choices: Faith, good deeds, truth, and patience
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Yes. Time is a gift, so we use it for good.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Try the answer with faith, good actions, truth, and patience.

quizQuestions.prompt: A good word can be counted as what?
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Yes. A gentle good word can be charity.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Try the answer that turns kindness into a good deed.
quizQuestions.source: Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim — a good word is charity

quizQuestions.prompt: What does the Qur'an teach about making fun of people?
choices: Do not mock or insult others
Only be kind to best friends
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Correct. Respect keeps the community safe and happy.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Choose the answer that protects people's feelings and honor.
quizQuestions.source: Qur'an 49:11 — do not ridicule or insult one another

quizQuestions.prompt: People are different nations and tribes so that we may...
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Yes. Our differences are a sign to learn and show respect.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Try the answer about meeting and respecting one another.
quizQuestions.source: Qur'an 49:13 — made into nations and tribes so you may know one another

quizQuestions.prompt: If you are not sure your words are helpful, what is a safe choice?
choices: Say the first thing in your mind
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Wise choice. Good speech or silence protects everyone.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Look for the choice that keeps your tongue gentle and safe.
quizQuestions.source: Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim — speak good or remain silent

quizQuestions.prompt: How should we treat people and animals in Allah's creation?
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Yes. Mercy is a beautiful Islamic character.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Try the answer filled with gentleness and care.
quizQuestions.source: Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim — Allah shows mercy to those who show mercy to people

quizQuestions.prompt: When something feels hard, what hopeful lesson does Surah Ash-Sharh teach?
quizQuestions.feedbackCorrect: Alhamdulillah. Allah reminds us that ease comes with hardship.
quizQuestions.feedbackIncorrect: Choose the hopeful reminder from the Qur'an.

collectibleLessons.name: Kind Words Lantern
collectibleLessons.pickupText: Kind words light up the village.
collectibleLessons.lesson: Before speaking, ask: Is it true? Is it kind? Is it useful?
collectibleLessons.source: Qur'an 2:83; Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim — speak good or remain silent

collectibleLessons.name: Knowledge Star
collectibleLessons.pickupText: A little star of learning joins your journal.
collectibleLessons.lesson: Say: Rabbi zidni 'ilma — My Lord, increase me in knowledge.

collectibleLessons.name: Mercy Seed
collectibleLessons.pickupText: Plant mercy and watch kindness grow.
collectibleLessons.lesson: Mercy means treating Allah's creation gently and helping when you can.
collectibleLessons.source: Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim — mercy shown to people

collectibleLessons.name: Helping Hand Token
collectibleLessons.lesson: Small helpful actions count, even moving harm from a path.
collectibleLessons.source: Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim — removing harm from the road is charity

collectibleLessons.name: Patience Cloud
collectibleLessons.pickupText: A soft cloud reminds you to breathe and try again.
collectibleLessons.lesson: Use time for faith, good deeds, truth, and patience.

collectibleLessons.name: Respect Flower
collectibleLessons.pickupText: A flower blooms when everyone is respected.
collectibleLessons.lesson: Do not mock others. Allah made people different so we may know one another.
